intro: The clearest split is that Ably lists entry pricing at $29 / month versus $500 per month for 100ms. Ably is Real-time messaging infrastructure with pub/sub, presence, and 99.999% uptime SLA across global PoPs; 100ms is Real-time / WebSocket Infrastructure tool for developers. The data points Ably at chat applications and live collaboration features, and 100ms at chat applications and live collaboration features. Pricing: Ably is subscription from $29 / month; 100ms is subscription from $500 per month. Lock-in is low for Ably and low for 100ms, with DX at 5/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 3/5. On listed strengths, Ably emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and 100ms emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Ably is for chat applications; 100ms is for chat applications; decide based on your entry-price budget.
verdict_a: Choose Ably if an entry price of $29 / month fits your budget and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose 100ms if an entry price of $500 per month fits your budget and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $29 / month for Ably and $500 per month for 100ms. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Ably to 100ms?
faq.a: Ably is tagged with low lock-in (proprietary pub/sub protocol and presence model). 100ms is tagged with low lock-in (proprietary real-time sdk with custom session model; migration requires rewrite). Both sides are low lock-in, so a migration is feasible but still needs planning around data export and SDK rewrites.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Ably or 100ms?
faq.a: The dataset rates Ably at 5/5 for developer experience and 100ms at 4/5, so Ably scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is 100ms a good alternative to Ably?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches chat applications, since both list the same fit. 100ms is flagged as not for static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
